page: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip and bzip3 single-threaded metrics reaching the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded decompression and database operations also score in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though larger uncached read and write operations perform at an average level. The system demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference tasks for small and medium models, though text generation for large 70B models falls into the average tier.

Given its high memory density and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources, the E48s_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local disks means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale Premium Storage independently. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database management systems, Redis caching layers, enterprise application servers, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 480 MB L3 cache and 384.0 GB of RAM. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). This server does not bundle local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU performance, top-tier single-threaded compression, and strong database and Redis operations. Large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance remain average. The E48s_v7 is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caches, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and strong single-core and database performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high RAM density for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration support
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, and LLM inference
